you.

And then we also did an audit of their on-page, off-page, and technical SEO, see where things are on the content front.

So I provided like 10 samples of where they can have a bit more authority.
I looked at their page search to see what keywords are really expensive.
To see how we can take advantage of it on an organic front.
And so that's where it really stemmed from, more than anything.
And so from there, I built the topic clusters, gave a sample article, built about three.

Creative operator.

Yes.

I think the project manager platform was really my life savior.

I would meet with them two days before the client call just to make sure we're on point with where we're at with our deliverables.
From that meeting, I send the client the agenda for our next call for a weekly call.
And it's about 24 hours in advance so that they can get back to me with any changes and I can allow the team time to, if anything new does come from the client, I have the team.
The team's allowed to at least modify whatever it needs that they need to do or have anything or prepare whatever they may need for the client call, you know.
And so if anything urgent needs to pop up, I'll do so.
I will communicate with them via Slack if any fires come up.
And then our project manager platform for everything.

It happens more frequently than not.
And so I think for me, it's one of my proudest moments because I felt like it was a proper team effort.

Yeah.
And then when you think about like learnings from that experience, is there anything that stands out as like.

With like production or with what?
With production.
With production more than anything.

I know it's because there was such high demand.

Yeah.

So having that focus there.
Yeah.
Yeah.

Who was managing all the workload across?

Based off hours spent.

But...
But...

kind of break it down in, like, the most elementary level.

Constructive feedback that I've received is improvement.
So I'm big on just telling the story, like, you know how storytelling for, like, reporting?
I'm big on, I'm a great storyteller, but I think the most recent feedback is to really not spend too much time on the minutiae, like, the little details, especially in regards to the timing of it.

So I think just keeping, like, tight, keeping everything as a tight ship and not really getting deep diving too much into it, especially when maybe irrelevant information, or not even irrelevant, because it doesn't.
Pertain to like the client's needs, right, as far as like what's being reported on, but if it doesn't really move the needle either, there's no need to like deep dive into it.
I think for me, it took place when I was kind of focusing on a lull, like I call them lulls because I don't like putting like, I think lulls is me placating, but just a little dip in performance.
I focused too much on it versus, I spent too much time focusing on like what took place there versus really deep diving into like the solution of it.
I had the solution, but there's like, given the amount of time that your client may have, structure the timing of it a bit better and what to focus on.
think because it does disrupt the flow of the story, if anything more so the friction, the experience of the story from the client's point of view.
Yeah, because little things like that versus spending too much time on a problem can really affect the overall account itself or the account health.
Yeah.
And so it was one time where my manager was kind of listening in.
And so it was great feedback.
Do you have any, like, techniques now to, like, stay ahead of that?
Because sometimes it's easy to, like, default back to old habits, right?
What do you do to kind of keep yourself in check?
I bullet point.

Like, I've reduced the amount of bullet points that I put, use for my, like, whenever I give reports and I prepare for it, I bullet point everything, not only just on the deck, but, like, I have what's on the deck, but I have, like, my little set of bullet points.
Yeah.
And so I manage it by how many bullet points I put in those areas and just having it focus on, like, honestly, high level.
I keep it high level unless the client asks for more details, right?
So I have the details there, but I'll, like, highlight what needs to be said.
And spoken about, and then I kind of just structure my practice.
I've restructured the way I prepare for my presentations in order to fix that problem.
Yeah, that's a good one.
Or just like, I sometimes put like the time in an agenda, like even for a week or two, just like five minutes.
Yes.
From 10 to 10.05, we're going to talk about this.
Yes.
Yeah, just something to like keep you.
Mm-hmm.
Um, and then like, what would you say is the impact on the client of doing that, doing a better job there?
Like what's the, what's the client, what does the client benefit from that?
I think the client benefits, it actually benefits, isn't an ignorance as bliss as almost as it sounds?
That's the first thing that comes to mind because I feel like it isn't, they're not really aware of it.
It's just so, it's such a reading between the lines sort of fix that, that's why I call it, I'm saying ignorance is bliss.
They don't really.
Oh, sorry.
I mean, what's the benefit of changing the behavior to being more, like, concise or to the point with the story?
Oh, it makes things a lot easier for the client to understand.
It avoids confusion on the client's front.
think being very concise, that's the thing for me, I'm really big on trying my very best to make things as elementary as possible.
And sometimes, like you said, my bad habits kind of leaning into over-explaining, right?
And so for me, the big win for the clients and, like, the benefit for them, like, there's, it has significantly reduced the confusion that they have had.
And I'll say on another end, it has helped me with recap notes, because what I'm writing down on my recap notes is essentially exactly what they heard, right?
And so that through line, it kind of triggers that memory as well for them.
Like, okay, yes, I remember this.
So having that retention, it reduces confusion and increases retention as far as what is being informed to them.
Yeah, I imagine all...
Also, it helps you because if, like, I feel like trying to get the customer to talk is, like, all the knowledge.
Like, there's so much valuable information just getting, asking the right questions versus telling.
So, like, the more you can get them talking and to give you, like, little glimpse of the information that might be helpful.
Absolutely.
Yeah.
Absolutely.
And that's my big thing.

As far as, a customer success stance goes for me, I like to build that sort of dynamic with the customer and the client because I realize, obviously, it has to be appropriate, but I feel like when you're able to ask those right questions, it drops that wall for them, too, in a way where, like, they view you as a partner, right?
And so even when things are shifting internally on their end versus you being the last one to know, you're right there with them, you know?
I've had a lot of accounts where they've been, because I've worked at a lot of startups where they've, like, ran out of, you know, they've ran out of budget, right?
And they haven't gotten there.
And that's on serious funding.
And so a transition is going to take place.
And so they've notified me right from beginning.
We're like, oh, like we have someone else coming in.
And I realized moments like that are essential.
It's coming from myself on the agency front.
Because when new people come in, that means chances are you may be getting tossed because they may have whoever they need, you know, whoever they have in mind to take over that role.

And so for me, I utilize that dynamic and that relationship, you know, them being comfortable with to let me know that information, to be ahead of the curb, set the meaning with the new individual to make sure we are still there to show them our value props, to show them what we have done.
I will build a whole deck to show how we quantitatively and qualitatively deserve to remain with them and keep this partnership afloat.
And I also let them know how much of a headache it is to bring someone new at the stage that we're in right now.
Yeah, that's fair.
And then what's a positive feedback?
Positive feedback.
Feedback.
Honestly, I'm really pleasant.
I don't know.
It's really weird.
Sometimes they're like, you have a great personality.
And I'll say it to this because I think it's a negative feedback that I've had earlier in my career, right?
Because I'm very outgoing and I'm really pleasant.
Um, clients have told my managers in the past, oh, we love her.
She's great.
But like during times where like bad news would be provided, like whenever I'm delivering like negative news, I'd have a smile on my face.
And it's not because, you know, I'm happy about it, obviously.
But I'm like, oh, I'm trying to, you know, customer service.
Just keep it on.
Like, it's fine.
Yeah.
So I utilize the smile as, I would utilize my smile as a means of, I would misdefine or misconstrue, like my smile as a means of giving the client confidence, like everything's going to be okay.
Yeah.
Well, for them, you know, they take it as like, no, like you're not taking it seriously.
And so for me, was a feedback, like you have a great personality and that's great.
Love you.
Clients do love me.
But I think it's like I said, like same thing with the whole timing of providing information, how long things should be discussed.
It was me.
And that's one thing I think is another one of my big wins as far as like personal performance goes, knowing how to deliver certain information or what demeanor I should have during client calls.
And so I think for me, that's from once I fixed that, that's when I kind of transitioned.
I became senior.
Yeah.
Sorry to cut you off.
I do have a hard stop because I have a call with lawyers and I know for every second they're waiting there.
It was so nice to meet you.
I'm sorry.
didn't have time.
Absolutely.
No worries.
We'll try to get back to you soon with next steps.
Absolutely.
If anything, Bridget, I'll shoot you an email with questions that I have.
And then we'll go ahead and move forward from there.
Thank you so, so much for your time.
Have a great one.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Interviewing Shaina Louis-Jean for the Engagement Manager role.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=327.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Proactive Upsell:** Secured a $5k/mo ARR expansion by proactively auditing a client's site for Meta's standards during a 6-month acquisition, creating a case study-worthy win.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
  - [**Systems-Driven:** Automated a personal tennis-coaching business to scale from one to three locations, demonstrating a core skill in building efficient, repeatable processes.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=466.0)
  - [**Actionable Feedback:** Improved client reporting by shifting from storytelling minutiae to high-level solutions, increasing clarity and retention.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1447.0)
  - [**Relationship-Focused:** Builds partnerships to gain early insight into client changes (e.g., budget shifts, new hires), enabling proactive defense of the agency relationship.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1764.0)

## Topics

### Professional Background & Philosophy

  - [**Current Role:** Automating a personal tennis-coaching business to focus on systems and efficiency.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=459.0)
      - [**Automation:** Built a customer feedback program and a referral program (30% off incentive) to drive growth.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=466.0)
      - [**Scaling:** Expanded the business from one to three locations (Kendall, Wynwood, Broward County) using geotargeting.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=560.0)
  - [**Agency Experience (829 & Beacon Digital):** Managed 10–12 mid-size to enterprise clients, each with a dedicated service "pod."](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=915.0)
      - [**Pod Structure:** Account Manager leads a team of specialists (Paid Media, SEO, Content, Creative).](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=890.0)
      - [**Workflow:** Internal team calls (2 days pre-client call) → Client agenda sent (24 hrs pre-call) for feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=963.0)

### Case Study: Proactive Upsell During Meta Acquisition

  - [**Context:** A mid-size client was acquired by Meta, creating a 6-month window of uncertainty where the agency's performance would determine contract renewal.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=757.0)
  - [**Strategy:** Proactively audited the client's site to meet Meta's enterprise standards.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)
      - [**UX Analysis:** Used Hotjar for a website breakdown → informed a partial site refresh.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=663.0)
      - [**SEO Audit:** Reviewed on-page, off-page, and technical SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=684.0)
      - [**Content Strategy:** Built topic clusters to capture expensive paid keywords organically, providing 10 samples and 3 full articles.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=694.0)
  - [**Outcome:** Secured a $5k/mo ARR expansion and a renewal with Meta, a win leadership used for a company case study.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=742.0)
  - [**Learning:** Trusting gut feelings on team workload is critical. Shaina's delayed intervention to assist an overutilized SEO operator caused preventable internal revisions.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1073.0)

### Client Management & Communication

  - [**Constructive Feedback:** Reports were too detailed, focusing on minutiae instead of high-level solutions.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1447.0)
      - [**Action:** Now uses bullet points and prioritizes high-level solutions, reserving details for client-requested deep dives.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1569.0)
      - [**Impact:** Reduces client confusion and improves information retention.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1681.0)
  - [**Positive Feedback:** Praised for a pleasant personality.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1855.0)
      - [**Past Misstep:** A smile used to convey confidence was misread as not taking bad news seriously.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1866.0)
      - [**Action:** Learned to adjust demeanor for different situations, a skill that coincided with a promotion to Senior Account Manager.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1866.0)

### Tool Proficiency

  - [**PM & Analytics:** ClickUp, Google Search Console, Looker Studio, Screaming Frog, SEMrush, Google Analytics.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1380.0)
  - [**Marketing & Sales:** HubSpot, Salesforce, Lift Relation.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1380.0)
  - [**AI & Scheduling:** ChatGPT, Motion (scheduling).](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1380.0)
  - [**Other:** Adobe, Asana, LinkedIn, Facebook.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1380.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Bridget McGillivray:** Follow up with Shaina Louis-Jean regarding next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1951.0)
  - [**Shaina Louis-Jean:** Email Bridget McGillivray with questions.](https://fathom.video/share/1xZ-J19xxgPRsSUAmxEHf9Uims1Uik9G?tab=summary&timestamp=1963.0)
